answers.answer: Helios Sentinel employs a multi-layered real-time threat detection engine that combines signature-based matching, behavioral analysis, and ML-driven anomaly detection, validated quarterly against the MITRE ATT&CK framework. Ingested data sources include endpoint telemetry (process events, file system changes, network connections), cloud workload logs (AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, GCP Audit Log), network flow data (NetFlow v9/IPFIX), and email gateway events — all processed at up to 50,000 events per second per tenant. Detection-to-alert latency averages 2.3 seconds for signature-based matches and 18 seconds for behavioral anomaly detections. The platform maintains a 99.7% true positive rate across its top 100 detection rules.

Helios Security currently holds the following compliance certifications: (1) SOC 2 Type II — most recent audit completed December 2024 by Deloitte; (2) ISO 27001:2022 — certified March 2024 by BSI; (3) FedRAMP Moderate Authorization — authorized June 2024, sponsored by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS); (4) HIPAA — last assessment October 2024, with a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) available; (5) PCI DSS v4.0 Level 1 Service Provider — validated September 2024 by Coalfire; and (6) StateRAMP — authorized January 2025. All certifications are maintained on a continuous monitoring basis, supplemented by quarterly internal audits, and audit reports are available upon request under NDA.

Helios Security's EPP+EDR bundle is priced per seat per month as follows: 500 endpoints at $18/seat/month ($108,000/year), 1,000 endpoints at $15/seat/month ($180,000/year, a 17% volume discount), and 5,000 endpoints at $11/seat/month ($660,000/year, a 39% volume discount). Volume discounts are automatically applied at each tier, and additional multi-year discounts are available: 5% for a 2-year term and 10% for a 3-year term. The minimum contract term is 12 months. Optional add-ons include SIEM integration (+$6/seat/month) and Managed Detection & Response (MDR) (+$12/seat/month); all pricing excludes professional services.

Helios Security currently serves 47 customers in the financial services vertical, spanning 12 banks, 8 insurance carriers, 15 asset management firms, and 12 fintech companies, with an average NPS of 72 in this sector. Three approved reference accounts are: (1) Meridian National Bank — 3,200 endpoints running EPP+EDR+SIEM, deployed since 2022; (2) Crestview Capital Partners — 850 endpoints running EPP+MDR, deployed since 2023; and (3) Apex Insurance Group — 5,100 endpoints on the full Helios platform, deployed since 2021.

Both searches converged on the same authoritative source. All required details are present. Composing the final JSON answer now.

issues.summary: Q5's answer is a raw model reasoning trace with embedded JSON rather than a clean answer, and its outer envelope shows confidence 'low' and an empty sources array while the inner JSON claims confidence 'high' and cites a specific source — creating a direct internal contradiction within the same record.
issues.recommended_fix: Replace the Q5 answer with the clean text extracted from the inner JSON object, set the outer confidence to 'high', populate sources with ['Helios Data Sovereignty & Privacy Whitepaper v3.1'], and remove the 'model output was not valid JSON' flag.

issues.summary: The financial services customer sub-counts (12 banks + 8 insurance carriers + 15 asset management firms + 12 fintech companies) sum to 47, which matches the stated total, but Apex Insurance Group is listed under financial services references yet insurance carriers are counted separately from fintech/asset management — this is consistent, however the sub-segment tally of 12+8+15+12 = 47 checks out exactly, so no arithmetic error exists; flagging instead that Q4 lists 12 fintech companies and 12 banks as equal counts, which reviewers may wish to verify is not a copy-paste duplication.
issues.recommended_fix: Verify with the Customer Success team that the bank count (12) and fintech count (12) are genuinely identical figures and not a duplication error before sending.

review.overall_assessment: The most serious issue is Q5, which was never properly formatted and contains conflicting metadata (low confidence, no sources) contradicting its embedded answer (high confidence, named source); this must be corrected before customer delivery. All other answers are numerically and factually consistent with one another, though the identical sub-counts in Q4 warrant a quick internal verification.
